Why You Can Trust RAND Research
Featured
Research
Six Ways AI Could Cause the Next Big War, and Why It Probably Won't
The likelihood of AI tools directly triggering a major war appears limited, but experts warn that there are specific pathways that could increase the risk of conflict if governments do not manage them properly.

Research
Feb 25, 2026
The Science and Practice of Proportionality in AI Risk Evaluations: AI Evaluations Should Provide Meaningful Risk Information Without Imposing Excessive Burden
Artificial intelligence evaluations should provide meaningful risk information without imposing excessive burden.
Research
Feb 23, 2026
Decisive Economic Advantage
Artificial general intelligence may differ from past technologies in ways that would allow a leader to turn an early advantage into decisive economic advantage.
Video
Feb 19, 2026
The Geopolitics of AGI: How Might Advanced AI Reshape Global Politics and Security?
How might advanced AI, including the potential emergence of artificial general intelligence (AGI), reshape global politics and security? Jim Mitre and Joel Predd discuss RAND's Geopolitics of AGI initiative and explore what policymakers need to know.
Research
Feb 12, 2026
A Systemic Risk Assessment Methodological Framework for the Global Polycrisis
As global crises interlink, systemic risks intensify. Drawing from recent food and energy shocks, researchers present a polycrisis framework that maps system architectures, explores transformative responses, and champions transparency and diversity.
Research
Feb 12, 2026
Robust Conservation Planning for Biodiversity Under Climate Change Uncertainty
Our conceptual study seeks the strengths and vulnerabilities of strategies across many possible future directions, facilitating both decisionmaking amongst strategies and emergence of robust and adaptive conservation plans.
Research
Feb 12, 2026
Supporting Climate-Related Decisions Under Uncertainty
One goal of climate science is often seen as reducing uncertainty. Robust decisionmaking can help those facing climate-related decisions to manage uncertainty through their choice of action rather than relying on science to reduce all relevant uncertainties.
Commentary
Feb 12, 2026
The Weapons of Mass Destruction AI Security Gap
The AI ecosystem may be too narrowly focused on a single threat model: the “lone wolf virus terrorist.” This emphasis on individual actors could leave state-based and terrorist group threats dangerously under-examined.
Research
Feb 11, 2026
Developing a Risk-Scoring Tool for Artificial Intelligence–Enabled Biological Design
Researchers examined five biological functions that could be modified using AI tools: altered host range or tropism, increased genome replication, immune or medical countermeasure evasion, increased environmental stability, and increased transmission dynamics.
Expert Insights
Feb 10, 2026
Measuring Biological Capabilities and Risks of AI Agents: Generating and Interpreting Evidence from Agentic Evaluations
This perspective examines biological agentic evaluations as a tool for assessing risks from increasingly autonomous AI systems and offers practical guidance on how evaluation design shapes conclusions about biological capability and risk.
Research
Feb 6, 2026
Position: Human Baselines in Model Evaluations Need Rigor and Transparency
Human baselines in foundation model evaluations must be more rigorous and more transparent to enable meaningful comparisons of human vs. AI performance. We provide recommendations and a reporting checklist towards this end.
Commentary
Feb 3, 2026
Tipping the Cyber Balance: How AI Benchmarks Could Make Software Safer
Automated reasoning can help protect critical software, build trustworthy infrastructure for AI systems, and lay the foundation for governing advanced AI.
Research
Feb 3, 2026
Bridging the Digital to Physical Divide: Evaluating LLM Agents on Benchtop DNA Acquisition
This report describes an automatable evaluation of eight frontier large language model (LLM) agents on their ability to design DNA segments, interact with a benchtop DNA synthesizer, and generate laboratory protocols.

RAND Global and Emerging Risks
Objective Analysis. Effective Solutions.
RAND is a research organization that develops solutions to public policy challenges to help make communities throughout the world safer and more secure, healthier and more prosperous. RAND is nonprofit, nonpartisan, and committed to the public interest.
